issues.description: The `dom: { isConnected: true }` property is copy-pasted into three separate mockView objects. If the guard condition evolves (e.g., checking additional DOM state), every mock must be updated independently.
issues.suggested_fix: Extract a shared `createMockView()` factory (or a base `mockViewDefaults` object) that includes `dom: { isConnected: true }` along with the other common mock properties (`posAtCoords`, `state`, `dispatch`), and spread/override per-test as needed.

issues.description: The `isConnected` guard correctly prevents `view.dispatch()` on a detached view, but it also silently suppresses the `onNavigate` callback for cross-file definitions. If the parent component is still alive but has swapped the editor (detaching the old view's DOM), a valid cross-file navigation would be silently dropped. This is likely fine given current usage, but worth documenting the intent.
issues.suggested_fix: Add a brief comment above the guard: `// View was destroyed (e.g., tab closed) before LSP responded; skip both dispatch and onNavigate.`

comprehensive_reviewer.summary: This is a focused, well-executed defensive fix. The production change adds a single `view.dom.isConnected` guard in the `.then()` callback of the detached LSP request promise, preventing `view.dispatch()` calls on a destroyed EditorView — a real race condition when the user Ctrl+clicks and then quickly closes/switches the editor before the LSP responds. The test changes correctly add `dom: { isConnected: true }` to existing mock views so the happy-path tests continue to pass the new guard. Dedicated negative tests for `isConnected === false` already exist in the test file (lines 272–349). No blocking issues found; two minor suggestions around test DRY-ness and a documentation comment on the guard's scope.
